prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sugarplum, the CPU is just the little busy-bee inside the computer box that does all the figuring. I picture it like the kitchen in a big diner: every time you click or type, it’s the cook who decides what recipe to follow and keeps the orders straight. It’s smaller than a saltine, but they say it has more “switches” than Grandma has buttons in her sewing jar. If you’re patient with it, that tiny cook can whip up a whole picture show or a letter to Aunt Lois quicker than I can frost a sheet cake.
